description:
## The change

Update pattern narrowing in `src/ir_typer.rs` so matching `[]` produces
`Descr::list_of(Descr::none())` (the lattice's empty-list witness)
instead of `Descr::nil()`. This collapses the spurious `nil | list(X)`
unions that pollute spec types today.

### Why

The lattice already has the right representation:

```rust
// src/types.rs:1990 (test, currently passing)
fn list_of_none_is_subtype_of_any_list() {
    // list(none) only contains nil, so it's a subtype of every list type.
    let nil_only = Descr::list_of(Descr::none());
    assert!(nil_only.is_subtype(&Descr::list_of(Descr::int())));
}
```

But `ir_typer`'s pattern narrowing on `[]` produces `Descr::nil()`,
not `Descr::list_of(Descr::none())`. The union arithmetic then produces
`nil | list(int)` for tail-narrowed types throughout. Today this
"works" because runtime conflates nil and []; after EL.2 it would be
wrong (a `nil | list(int)` spec would mean "accepts either the nil
atom OR a list," but only `[]` was ever intended).

### Change

  - In ir_typer's pattern narrowing for `[]` pattern: produce
    `Descr::list_of(Descr::none())` as the narrow type, not
    `Descr::nil()`.
  - Symmetrically, list-tail narrowing on `[h | t]` produces `t :
    list(elem)` — already includes the empty list via list(none)
    being a subtype; no `| nil` union needed.

### REMOVES

  - Spurious `nil |` artifacts in spec types throughout the codebase.
  - The user-facing confusion of "why does my length() function's
    signature mention nil when I never said nil anywhere?"

## Expected effect

  - Spec dumps change extensively:
      reverse_acc(nil | list(1|2|3|4|5), ...) → reverse_acc(list(1|2|3|4|5), ...)
      foldl(..., int, nil | list(1|2|3|4|5))  → foldl(..., int, list(1|2|3|4|5))
      k_21(int, nil | list(int), 1)            → k_21(int, list(int), 1)
  - Re-bless `expected.specs` across affected fixtures.
  - CLIF spec annotations re-bless.
  - `expected.txt` unchanged.
  - All 842 tests pass.
  - Spec dump sizes shrink modestly.

## Depends on

EL.2 (the runtime split has to land first; if narrowing used
`list_of(none())` before runtime split, the resulting types would
disagree with the runtime's still-conflated semantics).

## Walkthrough hazards

  - Find the precise narrowing site. Search for where `Pattern::List`
    or `Pattern::Nil` causes Descr narrowing in ir_typer (likely in
    type_fn's block-env narrowing or pattern_check.rs's matrix
    walking).
  - Tests asserting spec types include `nil |`: need updates.
  - Any caller that constructs `Descr::nil()` to mean "empty list"
    needs a sweep. Grep for `Descr::nil()` and audit intent.
title: fz-EL.3: typer pattern-narrowing simplification
